page: Amazon Web Services r6i.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The instance provides 8 virtual CPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPU accelerators are bundled with this instance type, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the r6i.2xlarge in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. The instance achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1628.0 and a multi-core score of 6046.0. Memory bandwidth tests show 413.39 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, while uncached large-block reads and writes reached 58.24 GB/sec and 42.08 GB/sec respectively. However, the Passmark memory score and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performance fall into the bottom 25% tier. For specialized workloads, the instance handles Redis SET operations at 7.96 million operations per second and small-model LLM inference at 172.46 tokens per second for text generation, though performance drops significantly on larger 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6i.2xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio suitable for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of excess compute cores. The dedicated CPU allocation and Intel Xeon architecture with AVX-512 support provide stable execution environments. This server is best suited for memory-bound workloads such as medium-sized databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, static web serving, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring a larger memory footprint.
description: Amazon Web Services r6i.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 8 vCPUs (4 physical cores) running at 3.5 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a high 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, but includes no local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Benchmark results place the instance in the average tier for most operations, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 6046.0 and Redis SET throughput of 7.96 million operations per second, though memory-intensive Passmark scores fall into the lower quartile. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that do not require massive parallel compute, making it well-suited for database operations, in-memory caching, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6i.2xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 3.5 GHz CPU, designed for database and in-memory caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6i.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, ideal for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for memory-intensive workloads.

64.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio
3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and static web serving
